heading: Drop a Stream Processor
body:
To drop a stream processor:

**Admin-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides an endpoint for
deleting a stream processor.

[Delete One Stream Processor](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deletegroupstreamprocessor)

**Atlas UI:**

Atlas UI

To delete a stream processor in the Atlas UI, go to the
**Stream Processing** page for your Atlas project
and click **Manage** in the pane for your stream processing workspace to
view the list of stream processors defined for it.

Then, click the **Delete** (trash-alt) icon for
your stream processor. In the confirmation dialog that appears,
type the name of the stream processor (`solarDemo`) to confirm
that you want to delete it, and then click **Delete**.

**mongosh:**

`mongosh`

To delete an existing stream processor with [mongosh](mongodb-shell), use the
[sp.processor.drop()](manual/reference/method/sp.processor.drop) method. It has the following syntax:

```sh
sp.processor.drop(<options>)
```

Where `<options>` is a generic, optional document whose fields are
passed to the underlying drop command.

For example, to drop a stream processor named `proc01`, run the
following command:

```sh
sp.proc01.drop()
```

This method returns:

- `true` if the stream processor exists.

- `false` if the stream processor doesn't exist.

When you drop a stream processor, all resources that Atlas Stream Processing
provisioned for it are destroyed, along with all saved state.
